skill.name: web-search-scraper-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract complete Markdown content from any website via the BrowserAct Web Search Scraper API. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like extract complete markdown from a specific website, scrape the content of an article link, get the text from a target url, convert a webpage to markdown format, fetch the main content of a blog post, extract data from a given web page, parse the html of a website into markdown, download the readable text from a news article, obtain the content of a tutorial page, extract all the markdown text from any http or https url, scrape documentation from a web link, or grab the text of a single webpage.
skill.summary: Extract complete Markdown content from any website via BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. All static findings evaluated as false positives or legitimate patterns. Network access is restricted to BrowserAct's official API endpoint. API key is handled properly and never sent to external destinations. No malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns detected. The skill is a legitimate web scraping API client for the BrowserAct service.

medium_findings.title: Network API Access Pattern
medium_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to external API endpoint with authentication headers. Verified as legitimate BrowserAct service usage - API key is only sent to https://api.browseract.com/v2/workflow, a known trusted endpoint. No data exfiltration detected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern is standard API client implementation - requests.post/get to BrowserAct API with proper Bearer token authentication. Intent is clearly documented web scraping functionality.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable API Key Access
medium_findings.description: Script retrieves BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv(). Evaluated as legitimate credential handling - key is used only for BrowserAct authentication and not exfiltrated to third parties.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard credential pattern - os.getenv() for API key with proper usage scope limited to BrowserAct API endpoint only.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs
low_findings.description: API endpoints hardcoded for BrowserAct service configuration. These are known, trusted endpoints documented in SKILL.md. Not suspicious - part of service configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate service configuration - URLs point to official BrowserAct domain. No dynamic URL construction from user input.

low_findings.title: Task Polling with Timeout
low_findings.description: Script polls API for task completion status with 300-second timeout. Standard API polling pattern for async operations - not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API polling implementation - task_id used for status checks, timeout is reasonable safety measure.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Blocks
low_findings.description: Markdown contains backtick syntax for code examples. Documentation artifacts detected by static scanner - not actual shell command execution risks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code blocks with bash examples - static scanner artifact, not actual command execution risks.

content.user_title: Extract Markdown from Any Website URL
content.value_statement: Web scraping often fails due to CAPTCHA, rate limits, or complex JavaScript rendering. This skill uses BrowserAct's API to reliably extract clean, complete Markdown content from any URL without these obstacles.
actual_capabilities: Extract complete Markdown content from any HTTP/HTTPS URL
Convert raw HTML pages into clean, readable markdown format
Bypass CAPTCHA and bot detection systems automatically
Handle JavaScript-rendered pages via browser automation
Retrieve article content, blog posts, documentation pages, and news articles
Process web content for AI analysis without hallucination risks
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Execution time varies based on page complexity (typically 10-60 seconds)
Cannot extract content from password-protected or login-required pages
Rate limits apply based on BrowserAct subscription tier

use_cases.title: Research Data Collection
use_cases.description: Automatically extract article content, documentation, and reference materials from multiple URLs for research purposes without manual copy-pasting.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and analysts who need to gather web content for studies

use_cases.title: Content Aggregation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Feed extracted markdown content into AI systems for summarization, analysis, or reformatting. Ideal for building content pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building automated content processing workflows

use_cases.title: Documentation Archival
use_cases.description: Download and archive technical documentation, tutorials, and API references in markdown format for offline access or backup purposes.
use_cases.target_user: Technical writers and DevOps engineers archiving documentation

prompt_templates.title: Extract Single Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the markdown content from this URL: ${url}
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a specific article or blog post link

prompt_templates.title: Batch URL Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web scraper skill to extract markdown from each of these URLs: ${urls}. Process them one by one and return the content.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides multiple URLs to scrape

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Scrape
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all content from the documentation at ${url} as markdown so I can read it offline.

prompt_templates.title: Content with Fallback
prompt_templates.prompt: Try to extract the article content from ${url}. If the API key is missing, ask me for it first before attempting the extraction.

output_examples.output: Successfully extracted 2,450 words of markdown content including all headings, code blocks, and formatted text from the article.
Converted 15 sections of documentation into clean markdown with preserved headings, lists, and code examples.

best_practices: Always verify the target URL is accessible before invoking the scraper to avoid unnecessary API calls
Handle missing API key gracefully by prompting user before proceeding
Implement retry logic (single retry) for transient failures, but stop on authorization errors
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted URLs without validation - the skill expects properly formatted HTTP/HTTPS URLs
Do not ignore API key errors - always report authentication failures to the user
Do not scrape the same URL repeatedly in quick succession - respect rate limits

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. Sign up at browseract.com to get your key.

faq.question: How long does extraction take?
faq.answer: Most pages extract in 10-60 seconds depending on complexity. The script polls for completion and reports status every 10 seconds.

faq.question: Can this bypass CAPTCHAs?
faq.answer: Yes. BrowserAct handles CAPTCHAs and bot detection automatically through browser automation.

faq.question: What formats are supported?
faq.answer: Any HTTP or HTTPS URL works. The output is always clean Markdown with preserved structure, headings, and code blocks.

faq.question: Is there a retry mechanism?
faq.answer: Yes. If a request fails without an authorization error, the agent will automatically retry once. Invalid API keys are not retried.

faq.question: Are there rate limits?
faq.answer: BrowserAct has rate limits based on your subscription tier. The skill is designed to respect reasonable usage patterns.
